title: Ortholoma illepidum
intro: Ortholoma illepidum, also known as Desert Honeysuckle, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including the treatment of fever, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Ortholoma illepidum include terpenoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The plant is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in topical applications such as salves and creams, to harness its health benefits.

constituents: Ortholoma illepidum contains compounds such as sesquiterpene lactones and phenolic acids. These compounds are thought to contribute to the plant's medicinal properties and may have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Further research is needed to fully understand the active constituents of Ortholoma illepidum. The plant's unique combination of bioactive compounds makes it a subject of interest in the field of pharmacognosy. However, more studies are required to confirm the exact composition and effects of these compounds.
